title: Cerasus conradinae
intro: Cerasus conradinae, also known as the Korean cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cerasus conradinae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cerasus conradinae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory problems.
